core_mechanism: A single scalar generating potential whose third derivatives serve as the structure constants of a commutative, associative product on the tangent spaces of a parameter manifold, with associativity imposed as an integrable system of nonlinear PDEs, and such potential-defined algebra structures studied up to structure-preserving isomorphism and under tensor (monoidal) composition
brief_reason: The paper compares three independent constructions of the same object (Frobenius manifolds), seeks isomorphisms between them (the Mirror Problem) and studies their tensor products, all of which presuppose the shared machinery of a potential-generated, associativity-constrained commutative-algebra structure on a manifold's tangent bundle.
